school.name: The Schoolhouse
school.description: Each teacher at the schoolhouse leads a different class in a different room. Work out who teaches what, and where.
clues: The class in Period 3's grade is higher than both Boyd's grade and the class in the Library's grade.
Period 5 goes with exactly two of Boyd, Studio, and 82%.
Hale, 82%, and 87% go with Period 2, Period 3, and Period 4, in some order.
Between Ames and Studio, one goes with Period 1 and the other with Period 5.
Period 4 goes with at least two of Hale, Cloister, and 77%.
Either Pavilion goes with Hale or Pavilion goes with Ellis.
The class with 92%'s period is lower than both Frost's period and the class in the Cloister's period.

chess.name: The Chess Club
chess.description: Regulars at the chess club each swear by a different opening, a favourite tactic, and a prized set. Work out who plays what.
clues: The player who placed 3rd's rating is higher than both Boris' rating and the London player's rating.
1st goes with exactly two of Boris, Slav, and 1789.
Nadia, 1789, and 1889 go with 4th, 3rd, and 2nd, in some order.
Between Anatoly and Slav, one goes with 5th and the other with 1st.
2nd goes with at least two of Nadia, French, and 1689.
Either Pirc goes with Nadia or Pirc goes with Magnus.
The 1989-rated player's placing is lower than both Mikhail's placing and the French player's placing.
